We matched them with a lender who offered a 12-month term at a competitive rate, secured on the property. The investor completed the purchase within 10 days, started the refurbishment immediately, and had the property listed for sale within four months. They achieved their target sale price, repaid the bridging loan in full, and walked away with a healthy profit. The entire process, from first meeting to completion, took just 12 days. Without bridging finance, they would have either missed the auction or forced a rushed sale of other assets.

How the Process Works

Step one is a simple conversation. You tell us what property you're buying, the purchase price, how much you need to borrow, and when you need the funds. We ask about your exit strategy (selling, refinancing, another property project) and your timescale. That takes 20 minutes.

Step two is lender matching. We take your details to our panel of bridging lenders and identify which ones will offer the best rate and terms for your specific situation. You'll usually have multiple offers within 2 to 4 business days.

Step three is your decision. You review the offers, ask any questions, and choose the lender that suits you best. We handle all the paperwork and liaison with the lender, your solicitor, and the seller's solicitor.

Step four is completion. Once your solicitor confirms funds are needed, the lender releases the money directly to your solicitor's client account, and you exchange and complete. From initial enquiry to funds in your account typically takes 7 to 14 days.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I get a bridging loan in Glasgow?
Yes. If you own property in Glasgow or can demonstrate a clear exit strategy (a property purchase, planning permission, mortgage in principle), we can arrange bridging finance. We work with borrowers from across Greater Glasgow, including Lanarkshire and East Ayrshire, so geography isn't a barrier.
How quickly can bridging finance be arranged in Glasgow?
Most Glasgow applications are funded within 7 to 14 days. We've completed faster when there's urgency and all information is ready upfront. The speed is one of the main reasons borrowers choose bridging loans over traditional mortgages.
What are the requirements for a bridging loan in Glasgow?
You'll need to demonstrate a clear exit strategy (the way you'll repay the loan), proof of the property purchase (offer letter or auction details), and usually an agreed valuation. You don't need to be employed or have a strong credit history in the traditional sense, though the property and exit strategy are what lenders focus on.
Does Boxx Commercial Finance arrange bridging loans in Glasgow?
Yes. We've been arranging bridging finance across Scotland for years, and Glasgow is one of our strongest markets. We know the local property landscape, the lenders who are active here, and the deal structures that work. We're not a call centre somewhere in the south. We understand Glasgow's property market firsthand.
What's the difference between an open and closed bridging loan?
A closed bridging loan has a fixed end date (usually your property sale completion date). An open bridging loan has no fixed end date, which is useful if you're unsure when your exit will happen. We explain both options and recommend whichever suits your situation. You can read more about closed versus open bridging loans on our insights page.
How much does bridging finance cost in Glasgow?
Interest rates vary depending on the lender, the amount borrowed, and how much equity you have in the property. Rates typically range from 0.5% to 1.5% per month, though we always provide a detailed illustration of costs before you commit. You can use our bridging loan calculator to estimate costs for your specific scenario.

If you're ready to move forward with a bridging loan in Glasgow, get a quote today by speaking with our team. We'll explain your options, match you with the right lender, and make sure you understand the full cost and timeline before we proceed. Get a bridging loan quote in Glasgow and see how quickly we can move. We've funded hundreds of Glasgow property transactions, and your situation will likely be familiar to us.
